PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
Off Calendar Jan-25-2010 Continued To May-14-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: This matter was called with case number PTR-07-290665 (The Look Lee Revocable Trust).

Appearances: George Engler for Liman Luo. Mary Sancimino for Kevin Lee (Trustee of The Look Lee Revocable Trust).

Ms. Sancimino stated that there was a second mediation, that a settlement was reached, that now Ms. Liman Luo does not wish to sign the settlement agreement, and that the matter should be set for trial. Mr. Engler concurred. Both Counsel agreed on a two hour time estimate. The Court set the matter for an evidentiary hearing on May 14, 2010 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204. The trial briefs are to spell out what is and what is not at issue and both briefs are due by the close of business on May 7, 2010. The Court stated some of the issues are: who the administrator will be, the spousal property petition, and what assets are in the Trust. The Court instructed Counsel to notify the Court by close of business on May 12, 2010 if the matter settles. Ms. Sancimino stated that if the matter settles she will file a petition to confirm the settlement due to the number of overseas beneficiaries.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR#2542. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy J. Capozzoli

PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
Off Calendar Sep-21-2009 Continued To Dec-21-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: This matter was called with case number 290665 The Look Lee Revocable Trust.

Appearances: Mary Sancimino for Kevin Lee (Successor Trustee). George Engler for Liman Luo (Surviving Spouse).

Ms. Sancimino stated that they mediated, that there were continuing settlement discussions, that they were missing a piece of discovery, that they were still tracing the Ameritrade account and requested a 90 to 120 day continuance. In addition, she stated that they were waiting for bank records, that they were trying to establish assets prior to marriage, that the Parties agreed that resuming mediation would be fruitful and that she was opposed to the Petition for Letters of Administration. The matter was continued to December 21, 2009 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for pursuing discovery and resuming mediation efforts with Ms. Johnson. If the matter is not resolved, it will be set for trial on that date.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR#2542. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Capozzoli.

Off Calendar May-11-2009 Continued To Aug-17-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Matter was heard with case #290665
Appearances: George J. Engler for and with Petitioner Li Man Luo, Mary Sancimino for and with Petitioner/Successor Trustee Kevin Lee.

The Court confirmed with counsel of the Pro-Bono Mediation, and for Ms. Sancimino to serve notices necessary to the beneficiaries in China.
Mediation must be complete no later than Aug. 11, 2009, Counsel to notify the Probate Examiner of the status of the mediation by Aug. 12, 2009.
The return date for Status Conference was set for Aug. 17, 2009 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204.
Minute Order referring case to Pro-Bono Mediation signed.

Comm. Sue Kaplan, CSR: LaVena Ward #7077, Clerk: S. Ravinantapricha, Bailiff: Deputy Gomez.

PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
Off Calendar Apr-14-2008 Continued To Aug-18-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: This matter was heard with case number 290665 (The Look Lee Revocable Trust).

Appearances: Mary Sancimino for and with Kevin Lee (Objector). George Engler for Liman Luo.

The Court granted the Petition for Appointment of Successor Trustee. Ms.Sancimino is to submit the order. With regard to the Spousal Property Petition and the Trust matter, the Court stated that issues needed to be resolved as to who owned the property, that the petitions would go to trial and that the parties would do discovery. The matters were continued to August 18, 2008 for status on discovery on the Spousal Property and the 850 petition.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR#11722. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy M. Lazatin.
